key_insight: Topology optimization can tailor plasmonic metasurfaces to maximize attractive optical forces and selectively trap nanoparticles.
engineering_relevance: The framework enables manufacturable planar metasurface designs for nanoparticle trapping in biosensing, microfabrication, and quantum system assembly.
method: Uses density-based topology optimization of plasmonic metasurfaces.
Computes nanoparticle optical forces with the Maxwell stress tensor under normal-incidence monochromatic illumination.
Compares free-form designs with manufacturability-constrained planar designs.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports optimized designs evaluated across different nanoparticle sizes and materials, showing higher trapping stiffness for smaller nanoparticles.
watch_item: Performance depends on nanoparticle size and material, so trapping may not generalize across all targets without retuning the topology.
paper_title: Topology optimized plasmonic metasurfaces for optical trapping of nanoparticles
